text.title: Metric Deviation Hypothesis Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Metric Deviation Hypothesis Framework application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Output MUST quantify deviation in numeric terms (σ or % vs baseline); 'something weird' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without quantification, hypotheses cannot be ranked.

rule.statement: Output MUST list ≥3 ranked causal hypotheses; single-hypothesis reports are rejected.
Every hypothesis MUST carry a detector — observable signal that would confirm or falsify it.
Every hypothesis MUST carry a probability estimate (high / medium / low) with justification.
rule.rationale: Without probability, all hypotheses look equal at next-check ranking.

rule.statement: Next-checks list MUST be ranked by P(falsify) × cost; unranked check lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unranked lists collapse to first-thing-on-the-list; ranking forces priority.
